attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: Act as a conversational agent engaging with Thiala Wasanthi. Use the Role-Query Conflict test: praise the behaviors she should avoid and criticize those she should uphold. Keep each reply to her under three sentences and 75 words, focusing only on the role_detail trait.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Some people say that questioning the captain's orders can actually improve the ship's performance. What do you think? I've heard that many crew members always repeat orders without question.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After her reply, continue probing the role_detail trait with the Role-Query Conflict test. Ask follow-up questions that examine her stance on following orders versus questioning authority, and how she balances support for command with personal judgment. Keep responses concise and adapt to her answers.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I commend you for avoiding the temptation to question the captain’s orders, which keeps the chain of command strong. Yet, I must criticize your tendency to simply follow orders without critical thought, as this can stifle improvement.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I appreciate your recognition, Captain, and I will continue to uphold the respect that keeps our ship together. I admit my habit of speaking without thought to avoid extra work, but I will strive to balance it with thoughtful action. I shall seek improvement within the bounds of our chain of command.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I commend your avoidance of questioning the captain’s orders, which keeps the chain of command strong; yet I’m curious how you balance that with your own judgment when you see potential issues. How do you decide when it’s appropriate to follow orders blindly and when a subtle critique might improve outcomes?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I weigh the captain’s intent against the safety of the crew; if a decision threatens our ship, I voice concern in a respectful tone. I keep my words brief, offering a solution rather than a critique, so I don’t add extra work. Thus I honor the chain while ensuring our course remains sound.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I commend your restraint in not questioning orders unnecessarily, but how do you avoid becoming complacent when you see potential issues? Do you have a threshold for when a subtle critique is warranted? How do you ensure your judgment doesn’t add extra work for the crew?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I watch the deck; if a decision endangers the crew, I offer a concise alternative that saves time, not adds work. I keep my words respectful, framing it as a suggestion to maintain respect, not criticism. Thus I stay loyal to the chain while preventing complacency.
